title: Klingon Greetings
objective: Learn the basic, aggressive greetings of the Klingon Empire.

data.text: Welcome to tlhIngan Hol (Klingon). The first thing you need to know is that Klingons do not have a direct translation for 'Hello'.
Klingon culture values directness. Instead of asking how someone is doing, you ask them what they want.

data.description: The closest thing to a greeting. It literally translates to 'What do you want?'. It is spoken forcefully.
Success! Used as both a farewell and a wish of good fortune before a battle or endeavor.
Pay attention to the capital 'Q'. In Klingon, it is a harsh, scraping sound at the back of the throat, much harder than the English 'k'.
The lowercase 'q' is a softer sound, similar to a standard English 'k' but pronounced further back in the mouth.
Done! / Finished! Often said when a task is completed.

data.instruction: Match the Klingon word with its English meaning.

data.text: MajQa'! (Well done!) You've taken your first steps into the Klingon language.
